data.description:
## What
Read-only derivations over git history for the code-health metrics with complete history: allowlisted-module count and total ceiling LOC, new vs raised ceilings, short-term churn, and refactor-to-addition ratio.

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py` — new: functions shelling to `git log`/`git diff` over `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` and the tree, registered.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py` — new.

## Approach
The ceilings ratchet already encodes the data: `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` (`<path> <max-lines>`) and `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` (bare paths) give the count + summed ceiling LOC by parsing; "new" vs "raised" is exactly a `git log -p` diff of the ceilings file (added `<path> <n>` row vs changed integer on an existing row); churn and refactor:addition come from `git log --numstat` (deletions:insertions) over the range. Deterministic and read-only.

## Tests
- Unit (real temp git repo): committing a new ceilings row then raising it classifies as one new + one raised; a numstat fixture yields the expected refactor:addition ratio.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py` passes against a temp git repo: total ceiling LOC equals the summed second column of a fixture ceilings file (`assert total == 806+1093`); allowlist count equals the fixture non-comment line count.
- [ ] a commit adding a ceilings row is classified `new` and a later commit changing that row's integer is `raised` — unit test asserts one of each.
data.title: Git-derivation readers (code-health metrics from git history)
